content.problemStatement:
JJ challenges the Chef to construct an array AAA of length NNN such that the following conditions hold:


AiA_iAi​ divides Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1​ when iii is odd and 1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N - 11≤i≤N−1
AiA_iAi​ does not divide Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1​ when iii is even and 1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N - 11≤i≤N−1
1≤Ai≤2⋅N1 \le A_i \le 2 \cdot N1≤Ai​≤2⋅N
All AiA_iAi​ are pairwise **distinct**
Can you help Chef complete JJ's challenge?

If multiple arrays satisfying the above conditions exist print any one of them.

It is guaranteed that under the given constraints, at least one array satisfying the above conditions exists.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA to be constructed.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing NNN space-separated integers, denoting the elements of the array AAA you constructed.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single integer N – the required length of the array.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output a line with N distinct integers A1 … AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 2·N) satisfying:
- If i is odd, Ai divides Ai+1.
- If i is even, Ai does NOT divide Ai+1.
All numbers must be space‑separated.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For N=4 the constructed array is [1,2,3,6];
- 1 divides 2 (odd index 1).
- 2 does not divide 3 (even index 2).
- 3 divides 6 (odd index 3).
All numbers are ≤ 2·4=8 and distinct.
For N=1 the array is [1] (trivially satisfies the conditions).
For N=6 the array is [1,2,3,6,5,10] and each adjacent pair follows the required divisibility pattern.

solution.approach:
Construct the array in pairs. For every odd index i (1‑based) set Ai = i. If i+1 ≤ N also set A(i+1) = 2·i. This guarantees:
- For odd i, Ai = i divides A(i+1) = 2·i.
- For even i, Ai = 2·(i‑1) is an even number larger than the next odd index value (i+1), therefore it cannot divide Ai+1 = i+1.
All numbers are distinct because odd positions contain odd numbers (the indices themselves) and even positions contain even numbers (twice an odd index). The maximum even value is 2·(N‑1) ≤ 2·N, satisfying the bound. The construction runs in O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the output array (no additional auxiliary memory).
